expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves donut-label assumptions for label visibility, density limits, and overlap-sensitive presentation
expectations.evidence: The output restates the case focus but does not show that context intake preserves the assumptions; it explicitly says this cannot be demonstrated: “No — not demonstrable with provided evidence.” and “the evidence set contains no stated assumptions or requirements about: when labels are visible vs hidden, limits/thresholds… how overlap/collisions are handled… Phase 1 artifacts… are not included.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: Both files frame the work as phase1 and describe the phase1 contract correctly: result.md: “Phase under test **phase1 (context intake / spawn planning)**” and “Phase 1 is limited to… Producing **`phase1_spawn_manifest.json`**… running `--post` validation… The orchestrator **must not perform phase logic inline**.” execution_notes.md similarly: “Phase1 per snapshot: spawn-manifest generation + post-validation; orchestrator should not inline logic.”
